mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if triggered periods show negative average forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still rising, the market prices tighter policy risk and lower real valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rising
failure_condition: Reject if the signal does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns during high-rate rising-inflation episodes, or if more than 60% of triggered cases are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend reflects weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not lower following UNRATE rises greater than 0.3pp in 3 months, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in recession-like labor deterioration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger final demand and housing-linked spending, which supports earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in RSAFS and HOUST does not coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and profit pressure from a slowing real economy, which should pull S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month declines in INDPRO fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent after controlling for broad market trend periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below a recent baseline signals a housing demand stall, which weakens cyclical growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST drops do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is no better than unconditional base rates in the same period.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains positive while UNRATE is stable or falling, payroll gains confirm durable income growth and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS growth with flat or falling UNRATE does not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship disappears once PAYEMS revisions are considered.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-hike stabilization phase, CPIAUCSL inflation that is flattening while FEDFUNDS stays unchanged reduces policy uncertainty and supports multiple expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if flat-to-lower inflation with unchanged FEDFUNDS does not improve 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect turns negative during the same regime.
